title: 10 best herbal teas for tinnitus
intro_desc: Herbal teas for tinnitus are natural remedies that have been used to alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus, a condition characterized by ringing or other sounds in the ears. These teas work by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and relaxing the nervous system, which can help to calm the constant noise and improve sleep quality. Examples of herbal teas that may help with tinnitus include Ginkgo biloba, St. John's Wort, and passionflower. For instance, drinking a cup of ginkgo biloba tea before bed may help reduce the severity of tinnitus symptoms, allowing individuals to sleep more soundly and wake up feeling refreshed and revitalized.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with tinnitus because it is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory and sedative properties. These compounds can help to reduce inflammation and tension in the auditory system, which are often linked to tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, the tea's ability to promote relaxation and calmness may also help to distract from tinnitus sounds, providing relief from its constant ringing or buzzing. Overall, maidenhair tree teas offers a natural and potentially effective way to alleviate the distress of tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair tree tea contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in maidenhair tree tea may help reduce swelling and irritation in the inner ear, which can alleviate tinnitus symptoms such as ringing or buzzing sounds.

Astringent properties: Maidenhair tree tea's astringent properties may help soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the ears, reducing inflammation and discomfort that can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ears, alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect the inner ear from damage and reduce tinnitus.
Seeds: The seeds are a rich source of essential fatty acids, which help to improve blood circulation and oxygenation to the ears, reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and two cups of boiling water.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for eight to ten minutes to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids or particles.
Mix one cup of the strained ginkgo biloba tea with one cup of cool boiled water and let it sit at room temperature for five hours.
Filter the mixture through a coffee filter paper or cotton cloth to produce a clear and potent ginkgo biloba herbal tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with tinnitus because of its unique properties that have been shown to alleviate symptoms of ringing in the ears. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been found to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system. By calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation, St John's Wort tea may help to quiet the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus, providing relief for those affected by this condition.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, contributing to relief from tinnitus symptoms.

Hypotensive action: St. John's Wort tea may help lower blood pressure, which is often linked to tinnitus, by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation to the ears, reducing ringing or buzzing sounds.

Adaptogenic effects: The adaptogenic properties of St. John's Wort tea may help the body adapt to stress, anxiety, and emotional changes that can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and reducing overall symptom severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in active compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it effective in soothing tinnitus symptoms.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried st john's wort flowers measuring one teaspoon per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the st john's wort in hot water for five minutes or until desired strength is achieved.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove loose herbs.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature for approximately three to five minutes.
Consume one to two cups of the herbal tea twice daily to support tinnitus relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi teas helps with tinnitus because it contains powerful herbs that calm the nervous system, reducing the perceived loudness of ringing sounds in the ears. The anti-inflammatory properties of brahmi help to alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for tinnitus. Additionally, brahmi's antioxidant properties protect the hair cells in the inner ear from damage, which can contribute to tinnitus. As a result, brahmi teas can provide relief from the constant ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds associated with tinnitus, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Brahmi tea's high antioxidant content helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Neuroprotective properties: The bioactive compounds in Brahmi tea, such as brahminoside and amyrin, have been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which may help protect the auditory system from damage and promote healthy hearing.

Anxiolytic and adaptogenic properties: Brahmi tea's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and adaptogenic effects can help alleviate stress-related tinnitus symptoms and promote a sense of calm, which can reduce overall discomfort associated with tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them an effective component in calming tinnitus symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, useful for soothing tinnitus-related discomfort and promoting overall ear health.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers or five to seven fresh ones.
Heat two cups of distilled water in a saucepan over medium heat for ten minutes.
Add the dried bacopa monnieri mixture to the boiling water and steep for twelve to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before consuming it immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cape jasmine teas helps with tinnitus because of its unique combination of active compounds that promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate anxiety. The tea's calming properties help to quiet the mind and calm the nerves, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals suffering from tinnitus who often experience heightened sensory sensitivity. Additionally, cape jasmine tea has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation in the inner ear, a common contributing factor to tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: The high levels of antioxidants in Cape Jasmine tea help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which are believed to contribute to the development of Tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Cape Jasmine tea can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the inner ear, which may alleviate symptoms of Tinnitus such as ringing or buzzing sounds.

Calming and sedative properties: The flavonoids and volatile oils in Cape Jasmine tea have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate Tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots have been traditionally used to soothe ear inflammation and reduce ringing sensations.
Barks: Containing alkaloids and phenolic acids, barks have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gardenia jasminoides flowers or use dried ones, taking 2-3 teaspoons of the herb.
Steep the gardenia jasminoides in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large cup or teapot.
Discard the solids and add honey to taste, using up to 1 tablespoon per serving as needed.
Allow the tea to cool down for at least 30 minutes before consuming it slowly throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with tinnitus because it contains compounds that have a soothing effect on the ear and auditory system. The tea's flavonoids and saponins help to reduce inflammation and irritation, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, maypop tea's natural relaxant properties can help to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate tinnitus. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, maypop teas can provide relief from tinnitus symptoms, improving overall hearing health and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Maypop tea contains antioxidants such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms caused by damage to inner ear hair cells.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties of maypop tea, including its ability to inhibit cyclooxygenase enzymes, can help to reduce inflammation in the inner ear and surrounding tissues, which is often a contributing factor to tinnitus.

Anxiolytic and sedative compounds: Maypop tea contains compounds that have been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative effects, which may help to calm the nervous system and reduce the perception of tinnitus symptoms, such as ringing or buzzing sounds.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Maypop contain a rich source of flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing tinnitus symptoms.
Fruits: The fruits of Maypop are known to possess sedative and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate the ringing sensation associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried passiflora incarnata flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired amount of tea.
Measure one teaspoon of dried herb per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the herb in hot water for 5-7 minutes or according to package instructions.
Strain and discard solids before serving warm or at room temperature.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with tinnitus because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that often exacerbates the condition. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also work to alleviate any underlying inflammation in the ears, while its sedative qualities promote a peaceful sleep, allowing for better rest and recovery. Additionally, lavender's ability to calm the nervous system may help regulate sound processing in the brain, providing relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of tinnitus.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: The calming compounds in lavender tea, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a soothing impact on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear or auditory nerve, which is thought to contribute to the development of tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English Lavender contain compounds like linalyl acetate and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe Tinnitus symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of English Lavender are rich in essential oils, particularly linalool, which has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress-related Tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried lavender flowers for use in tea preparation.
Measure one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers into a heat-resistant cup.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the dried lavender flowers and let steep.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and discarding solids.
Strain the liquid into a separate container and drink immediately, using 2-3 cups per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with tinnitus because its calming properties can alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often linked to the condition. The tea's sedative effects can also help regulate the brain's activity patterns, reducing the perceived ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. Additionally, valerian has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe damaged ear tissues and reduce inflammation in the auditory system, further contributing to its potential benefits for tinnitus sufferers.
remedy_properties: GABA-mimetic effects: Valerian's GABA-like compounds help increase the activity of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), a neurotransmitter that calms down overactive nerve cells, which can contribute to tinnitus.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Valerian tea's anxiolytic and sedative effects help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for tinnitus symptoms, allowing the individual to relax and potentially alleviate their tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: Valerian's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus development or exacerbation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Valeriana officinalis plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess sedative and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing the symptoms of tinnitus.

Rhyzomes: Valerian rhizomes are rich in valerenic acid, a compound that has been found to have anticonvulsant and sedative effects, helping to calm the nervous system and alleviate tinnitus-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried roots of valeriana officinalis, crush them into fine powder, and store in an airtight container for future use.
Boil one teaspoon of crushed valeriana roots in one cup of distilled water for 10-15 minutes to create the tea solution.
Strain the tea solution through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Mix the liquid extract with honey to taste and let it cool to room temperature before storing in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.
Drink one cup of the valeriana tea solution twice daily, 30 minutes after meals, for a minimum of three weeks to alleviate tinnitus symptoms effectively.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with tinnitus because it possesses a unique combination of properties that soothe and calm the auditory system. The tea's natural calming agents, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, work to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are often triggers for tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, lemon balm's sedative effects can help to quiet the mind and promote relaxation, allowing individuals to focus on other sounds rather than their ringing ears. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects: Lemon balm's calming and soothing properties can help to alleviate anxiety and stress-related tinnitus, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing ear noise perceptions.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves or 3 tablespoons of fresh leaves for each 8-ounce cup of water.
Measure 200 milliliters of boiling water and pour it over the herbal material in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the bioactive compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for at least 2 hours before drinking it once daily for optimal relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange teas helps with tinnitus because it contains limonene, a compound that has been shown to have a profound effect on alleviating ringing in the ears. The tea's bitter flavor is due to the presence of alkaloids like citral and hesperidin, which also possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work synergistically to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, helping to calm and soothe the auditory system and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Bitter orange tea contains flavonoids such as naringenin and hesperidin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear and potentially alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Volatile oils: The tea's volatile oils, including limonene and citral, may help to reduce noise-induced hearing loss and tinnitus by protecting the hair cells in the cochlea from damage caused by loud sounds or ototoxins.

Citric acid: Citric acid in bitter orange tea may have a vasodilatory effect, increasing blood flow to the inner ear and potentially helping to reduce tinnitus symptoms by improving auditory nerve function and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, roots are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
Seeds: Containing a high amount of citric acid, seeds have been traditionally used to treat ear infections and inflammation, which can contribute to tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried citrus x aurantium bark per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavonoids and limonoids to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add honey to taste if desired and stir well to combine with the herbal extract.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consumption to enhance bioavailability.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon teas helps with tinnitus because of its unique properties that have been found to be effective in alleviating ringing sounds in the ears. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, help reduce inflammation in the auditory system, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms. remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried cinnamomum verum bark for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat the water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil for approximately five minutes.
Add the measured cinnamomum verum bark to the boiling water and let it steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the loose herbal material.
Drink the warm or cooled tea immediately, aiming to consume three to four cups per day throughout treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with tinnitus because its soothing properties can calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress that often exacerbate the condition. The apigenin in chamomile interacts with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and tranquility. This calming effect can help distract from the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus, allowing individuals to find peace and quiet amidst the noise. Hypnotic properties: Chamomile tea's sedative effects can help improve sleep quality, as poor sleep is often a common issue for individuals with tinnitus. Improved sleep can, in turn, reduce tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them a popular choice for soothing tinnitus symptoms.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain bisabolol, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried chamomile flowers weighing about two teaspoons per cup.
Heat fresh water in a pot or kettle to a rolling boil for five minutes.
Add the dried chamomile flowers to the boiling water and steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming within thirty to sixty minutes of preparation.

Pour the hot water over the scutellaria baicalensis root powder and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the teapot's built-in strainer or a separate tea infuser.
Drink the tea immediately, consuming one to two cups three times daily for optimal results.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried tilia cordata leaves measuring one to two teaspoons for each cup of boiling water.
Combine the tilia cordata leaves with boiling water in a teapot or heat-resistant cup allowing it to steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or cheesecloth discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then sweeten with honey if desired and enjoy immediately while warm.
Repeat this process two to three times daily as needed adjusting dosage and steeping time based on individual tolerance.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried agrimonia eupatoria leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured herb and water in a medium-sized pot and bring to a boil over high heat.

remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried dandelion roots taraxacum officinale into a heat-resistant cup.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 100°c and pour it over the herbs in the cup.

Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rose petals rosa damascena for a minimum of one cup.

Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean teapot and discard solids.

Serve chilled tea in small amounts three to four times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with tinnitus because it has natural calming properties that can soothe the nerves and reduce stress, common triggers of tinnitus symptoms. The menthol in peppermint tea also helps to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the inner ear, which can alleviate ringing sensations and buzzing sounds. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the ear, further contributing to its relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Peppermint tea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and worsening of tinnitus symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint tea, such as rosmarinic acid, may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is believed to play a role in many cases of tinnitus.

Calming effects on the nervous system: Peppermint tea's calming and soothing properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for tinnitus episodes. The tea's ability to calm the nervous system may also help alleviate symptoms such as ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain bioactive compounds like menthol and menthone, which help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation and relieving anxiety.
Barks: Peppermint barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the ringing sensation in the ears associated with tinnitus, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 fresh peppermint leaves and rinse them gently with cold running water.
Chop the leaves finely to release their essential oils and flavor compounds instantly.
Steep the chopped mint leaves in boiling water for exactly 5-7 minutes to extract the active constituents thoroughly.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup to remove any remaining solids efficiently.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it slowly and deliberately over the next hour.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat tinnitus with herbal teas is by incorporating soothing and calming botanicals into your daily routine. Try drinking peppermint, chamomile, or valerian root tea regularly to alleviate symptoms. These herbs have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce ear pressure and calm the nervous system, providing relief from ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to tinnitus that are treated with herbal teas are hearing loss, ear infections, and Meniere's disease. Herbal teas such as Ginkgo biloba, St. John's Wort, and licorice root tea have been used to improve hearing clarity and reduce ear inflammation. Other herbal teas like peppermint and ginger tea can help alleviate symptoms of vertigo and dizziness associated with Meniere's disease. Additionally, Echinacea tea is sometimes used to boost the immune system and reduce ear infections.
